title: Check Point Gateway CPU and Memory Health
spl:
index=metrics (sourcetype="cp:perf" OR sourcetype="cp:snmp")
| stats avg(cpu_util) as cpu, avg(mem_pct) as mem by gw_name
| where cpu>90 OR mem>92
description: Gateway health prevents inspection bypass under load and connection drops.
value: Gateway health prevents inspection bypass under load and connection drops.
implementation: Stream gateway performance metrics. Correlate with inspection load and connection spikes.
visualization: Single value, Line chart (CPU/mem), Table (top gateways).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) as agg_value from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Check Point blocks from known pen tests, SandBlast detonations, or temporary policy or identity exceptions; confirm in SmartConsole before treating as a new campaign.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cp:perf`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Stream gateway performance metrics. Correlate with inspection load and connection spikes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=metrics (sourcetype="cp:perf" OR sourcetype="cp:snmp")
| stats avg(cpu_util) as cpu, avg(mem_pct) as mem by gw_name
| where cpu>90 OR mem>92
```

Understanding this SPL

**Check Point Gateway CPU and Memory Health** — Gateway health prevents inspection bypass under load and connection drops.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp:perf`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: metrics; **sourcetype**: cp:perf, cp:snmp. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=metrics, sourcetype="cp:perf". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by gw_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where cpu>90 OR mem>92` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) as agg_value from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Check Point Gateway CPU and Memory Health** — Gateway health prevents inspection bypass under load and connection drops.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp:perf`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Performance.CPU` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor’s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value, Line chart (CPU/mem), Table (top gateways).
grandmaExplanation: We use Check Point data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "Check Point Gateway CPU and Memory Health". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Performance.CPU` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
